verses.latin_vulgate: Quod factum est verbum Domini ad Jeremiam prophetam contra gentes.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲשֶׁ֨ר הָיָ֧ה דְבַר־יְהֹוָ֛ה אֶל־יִרְמְיָ֥הוּ הַנָּבִ֖יא עַל־הַגּוֹיִֽם׃
verses.king_james: The word of the Lord which came to Jeremiah the prophet against the Gentiles;

verses.latin_vulgate: Ad Ægyptum, adversum exercitum Pharaonis Nechao regis Ægypti, qui erat juxta fluvium Euphraten in Charcamis, quem percussit Nabuchodonosor rex Babylonis, in quarto anno Joakim filii Josiæ regis Juda.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְמִצְרַ֗יִם עַל־חֵ֨יל פַּרְעֹ֤ה נְכוֹ֙ מֶ֣לֶךְ מִצְרַ֔יִם אֲשֶׁר־הָיָ֥ה עַל־נְהַר־פְּרָ֖ת בְּכַרְכְּמִ֑שׁ אֲשֶׁ֣ר הִכָּ֗ה נְבֽוּכַדְרֶאצַּר֙ מֶ֣לֶךְ בָּבֶ֔ל בִּשְׁנַת֙ הָרְבִיעִ֔ית לִיהוֹיָקִ֥ים בֶּן־יֹאשִׁיָּ֖הוּ מֶ֥לֶךְ יְהוּדָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Against Egypt, against the army of Pharaoh–necho king of Egypt, which was by the river Euphrates in Carchemish, which Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon smote in the fourth year of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah king of Judah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Præparate scutum et clypeum,  et procedite ad bellum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עִרְכ֤וּ מָגֵן֙ וְצִנָּ֔ה וּגְשׁ֖וּ לַמִּלְחָמָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Order ye the buckler and shield, and draw near to battle.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jungite equos, et ascendite, equites :  state in galeis, polite lanceas, induite vos loricis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִסְר֣וּ הַסּוּסִ֗ים וַֽעֲלוּ֙ הַפָּ֣רָשִׁ֔ים וְהִֽתְיַצְּב֖וּ בְּכוֹבָעִ֑ים מִרְקוּ֙ הָרְמָחִ֔ים לִבְשׁ֖וּ הַסִּרְיֹנֹֽת׃
verses.king_james: Harness the horses; and get up, ye horsemen, and stand forth with your helmets; furbish the spears, and put on the brigandines.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid igitur ? vidi ipsos pavidos,  et terga vertentes,  fortes eorum cæsos :  fugerunt conciti, nec respexerunt :  terror undique, ait Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַדּ֣וּעַ רָאִ֗יתִי הֵ֣מָּה חַתִּים֮ נְסֹגִ֣ים אָחוֹר֒ וְגִבּוֹרֵיהֶ֣ם יֻכַּ֔תּוּ וּמָנ֥וֹס נָ֖סוּ וְלֹ֣א הִפְנ֑וּ מָג֥וֹר מִסָּבִ֖יב נְאֻם־יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Wherefore have I seen them dismayed and turned away back? and their mighty ones are beaten down, and are fled apace, and look not back: for fear was round about, saith the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non fugiat velox,  nec salvari se putet fortis :  ad aquilonem juxta flumen Euphraten  victi sunt, et ruerunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־יָנ֣וּס הַקַּ֔ל וְאַל־יִמָּלֵ֖ט הַגִּבּ֑וֹר צָפ֙וֹנָה֙ עַל־יַ֣ד נְהַר־פְּרָ֔ת כָּשְׁל֖וּ וְנָפָֽלוּ׃
verses.king_james: Let not the swift flee away, nor the mighty man escape; they shall stumble, and fall toward the north by the river Euphrates.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quis est iste, qui quasi flumen ascendit,  et veluti fluviorum intumescunt gurgites ejus ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִי־זֶ֖ה כַּיְאֹ֣ר יַעֲלֶ֑ה כַּנְּהָר֕וֹת יִֽתְגָּעֲשׁ֖וּ מֵימָֽיו׃
verses.king_james: Who is this that cometh up as a flood, whose waters are moved as the rivers?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ægyptus fluminis instar ascendit,  et velut flumina movebuntur fluctus ejus,  et dicet : Ascendens operiam terram :  perdam civitatem, et habitatores ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִצְרַ֙יִם֙ כַּיְאֹ֣ר יַעֲלֶ֔ה וְכַנְּהָר֖וֹת יִתְגֹּ֣עֲשׁוּ מָ֑יִם וַיֹּ֗אמֶר אַֽעֲלֶה֙ אֲכַסֶּה־אֶ֔רֶץ אֹבִ֥ידָה עִ֖יר וְיֹ֥שְׁבֵי בָֽהּ׃
verses.king_james: Egypt riseth up like a flood, and his waters are moved like the rivers; and he saith, I will go up, and will cover the earth; I will destroy the city and the inhabitants thereof.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ascendite equos,  et exsultate in curribus,  et procedant fortes,  Æthiopia et Libyes tenentes scutum,  et Lydii arripientes et jacientes sagittas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֲל֤וּ הַסּוּסִים֙ וְהִתְהֹלְל֣וּ הָרֶ֔כֶב וְיֵצְא֖וּ הַגִּבּוֹרִ֑ים כּ֤וּשׁ וּפוּט֙ תֹּפְשֵׂ֣י מָגֵ֔ן וְלוּדִ֕ים תֹּפְשֵׂ֖י דֹּ֥רְכֵי קָֽשֶׁת׃
verses.king_james: Come up, ye horses; and rage, ye chariots; and let the mighty men come forth; the Ethiopians and the Libyans, that handle the shield; and the Lydians, that handle and bend the bow.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dies autem ille Domini Dei exercituum dies ultionis,  ut sumat vindictam de inimicis suis :  devorabit gladius, et saturabitur,  et inebriabitur sanguine eorum :  victima enim Domini Dei exercituum  in terra aquilonis juxta flumen Euphraten.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְֽהַיּ֨וֹם הַה֜וּא לַֽאדֹנָ֧י יֱהֹוִ֣ה צְבָא֗וֹת י֤וֹם נְקָמָה֙ לְהִנָּקֵ֣ם מִצָּרָ֔יו וְאָכְלָ֥ה חֶ֙רֶב֙ וְשָׂ֣בְעָ֔ה וְרָוְתָ֖ה מִדָּמָ֑ם כִּ֣י זֶ֠בַח לַאדֹנָ֨י יֱהֹוִ֧ה צְבָא֛וֹת בְּאֶ֥רֶץ צָפ֖וֹן אֶל־נְהַר־פְּרָֽת׃
verses.king_james: For this is the day of the Lord God of hosts, a day of vengeance, that he may avenge him of his adversaries: and the sword shall devour, and it shall be satiate and made drunk with their blood: for the Lord God of hosts hath a sacrifice in the north country by the river Euphrates.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ascende in Galaad, et tolle resinam,  virgo filia Ægypti :  frustra multiplicas medicamina :  sanitas non erit tibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֲלִ֤י גִלְעָד֙ וּקְחִ֣י צֳרִ֔י בְּתוּלַ֖ת בַּת־מִצְרָ֑יִם לַשָּׁוְא֙ (הרביתי) [הִרְבֵּ֣ית] רְפֻא֔וֹת תְּעָלָ֖ה אֵ֥ין לָֽךְ׃
verses.king_james: Go up into Gilead, and take balm, O virgin, the daughter of Egypt: in vain shalt thou use many medicines; for thou shalt not be cured.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audierunt gentes ignominiam tuam,  et ululatus tuus replevit terram :  quia fortis impegit in fortem,  et ambo pariter conciderunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שָׁמְע֤וּ גוֹיִם֙ קְלוֹנֵ֔ךְ וְצִוְחָתֵ֖ךְ מָלְאָ֣ה הָאָ֑רֶץ כִּֽי־גִבּ֤וֹר בְּגִבּוֹר֙ כָּשָׁ֔לוּ יַחְדָּ֖יו נָפְל֥וּ שְׁנֵיהֶֽם׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: The nations have heard of thy shame, and thy cry hath filled the land: for the mighty man hath stumbled against the mighty, and they are fallen both together.

verses.latin_vulgate: Verbum quod locutus est Dominus ad Jeremiam prophetam, super eo quod venturus esset Nabuchodonosor rex Babylonis, et percussurus terram Ægypti :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַדָּבָר֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר דִּבֶּ֣ר יְהֹוָ֔ה אֶֽל־יִרְמְיָ֖הוּ הַנָּבִ֑יא לָב֗וֹא נְבֽוּכַדְרֶאצַּר֙ מֶ֣לֶךְ בָּבֶ֔ל לְהַכּ֖וֹת אֶת־אֶ֥רֶץ מִצְרָֽיִם׃
verses.king_james: The word that the Lord spake to Jeremiah the prophet, how Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon should come and smite the land of Egypt.

verses.latin_vulgate: Annuntiate Ægypto, et auditum facite in Magdalo,  et resonet in Memphis, et in Taphnis :  dicite : Sta, et præpara te,  quia devorabit gladius ea quæ per circuitum tuum sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַגִּ֤ידוּ בְמִצְרַ֙יִם֙ וְהַשְׁמִ֣יעֽוּ בְמִגְדּ֔וֹל וְהַשְׁמִ֥יעוּ בְנֹ֖ף וּבְתַחְפַּנְחֵ֑ס אִמְר֗וּ הִתְיַצֵּב֙ וְהָכֵ֣ן לָ֔ךְ כִּֽי־אָכְלָ֥ה חֶ֖רֶב סְבִיבֶֽיךָ׃
verses.king_james: Declare ye in Egypt, and publish in Migdol, and publish in Noph and in Tahpanhes: say ye, Stand fast, and prepare thee; for the sword shall devour round about thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quare computruit fortis tuus ?  non stetit, quoniam Dominus subvertit eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַדּ֖וּעַ נִסְחַ֣ף אַבִּירֶ֑יךָ לֹ֣א עָמַ֔ד כִּ֥י יְהֹוָ֖ה הֲדָפֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Why are thy valiant men swept away? they stood not, because the Lord did drive them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Multiplicavit ruentes,  ceciditque vir ad proximum suum, et dicent :  Surge, et revertamur ad populum nostrum,  et ad terram nativitatis nostræ,  a facie gladii columbæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִרְבָּ֖ה כּוֹשֵׁ֑ל גַּם־נָפַ֞ל אִ֣ישׁ אֶל־רֵעֵ֗הוּ וַיֹּֽאמְרוּ֙ ק֣וּמָה ׀ וְנָשֻׁ֣בָה אֶל־עַמֵּ֗נוּ וְאֶל־אֶ֙רֶץ֙ מוֹלַדְתֵּ֔נוּ מִפְּנֵ֖י חֶ֥רֶב הַיּוֹנָֽה׃
verses.king_james: He made many to fall, yea, one fell upon another: and they said, Arise, and let us go again to our own people, and to the land of our nativity, from the oppressing sword.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vocate nomen Pharaonis regis Ægypti :  tumultum adduxit tempus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קָרְא֖וּ שָׁ֑ם פַּרְעֹ֤ה מֶֽלֶךְ־מִצְרַ֙יִם֙ שָׁא֔וֹן הֶעֱבִ֖יר הַמּוֹעֵֽד׃
verses.king_james: They did cry there, Pharaoh king of Egypt is but a noise; he hath passed the time appointed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vivo ego, inquit Rex (Dominus exercituum nomen ejus),  quoniam sicut Thabor in montibus,  et sicut Carmelus in mari, veniet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חַי־אָ֙נִי֙ נְאֻם־הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ יְהֹוָ֥ה צְבָא֖וֹת שְׁמ֑וֹ כִּ֚י כְּתָב֣וֹר בֶּהָרִ֔ים וּכְכַרְמֶ֖ל בַּיָּ֥ם יָבֽוֹא׃
verses.king_james: As I live, saith the King, whose name is the Lord of hosts, Surely as Tabor is among the mountains, and as Carmel by the sea, so shall he come.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vasa transmigrationis fac tibi,  habitatrix filia Ægypti :  quia Memphis in solitudinem erit,  et deseretur, et inhabitabilis erit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כְּלֵ֤י גוֹלָה֙ עֲשִׂ֣י לָ֔ךְ יוֹשֶׁ֖בֶת בַּת־מִצְרָ֑יִם כִּי־נֹף֙ לְשַׁמָּ֣ה תִֽהְיֶ֔ה וְנִצְּתָ֖ה מֵאֵ֥ין יוֹשֵֽׁב׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: O thou daughter dwelling in Egypt, furnish thyself to go into captivity: for Noph shall be waste and desolate without an inhabitant.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vitula elegans atque formosa Ægyptus,  stimulator ab aquilone veniet ei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֶגְלָ֥ה יְפֵה־פִיָּ֖ה מִצְרָ֑יִם קֶ֥רֶץ מִצָּפ֖וֹן בָּ֥א בָֽא׃
verses.king_james: Egypt is like a very fair heifer, but destruction cometh; it cometh out of the north.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mercenarii quoque ejus,  qui versabantur in medio ejus quasi vituli saginati,  versi sunt, et fugerunt simul,  nec stare potuerunt :  quia dies interfectionis eorum venit super eos,  tempus visitationis eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּם־שְׂכִרֶ֤יהָ בְקִרְבָּהּ֙ כְּעֶגְלֵ֣י מַרְבֵּ֔ק כִּֽי־גַם־הֵ֧מָּה הִפְנ֛וּ נָ֥סוּ יַחְדָּ֖יו לֹ֣א עָמָ֑דוּ כִּ֣י י֥וֹם אֵידָ֛ם בָּ֥א עֲלֵיהֶ֖ם עֵ֥ת פְּקֻדָּתָֽם׃
verses.king_james: Also her hired men are in the midst of her like fatted bullocks; for they also are turned back, and are fled away together: they did not stand, because the day of their calamity was come upon them, and the time of their visitation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vox ejus quasi æris sonabit :  quoniam cum exercitu properabunt,  et cum securibus venient ei  quasi cædentes ligna.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קוֹלָ֖הּ כַּנָּחָ֣שׁ יֵלֵ֑ךְ כִּֽי־בְחַ֣יִל יֵלֵ֔כוּ וּבְקַרְדֻּמּוֹת֙ בָּ֣אוּ לָ֔הּ כְּחֹטְבֵ֖י עֵצִֽים׃
verses.king_james: The voice thereof shall go like a serpent; for they shall march with an army, and come against her with axes, as hewers of wood.

verses.latin_vulgate: Succiderunt saltum ejus, ait Dominus,  qui supputari non potest :  multiplicati sunt super locustas,  et non est eis numerus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כָּרְת֤וּ יַעְרָהּ֙ נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֔ה כִּ֖י לֹ֣א יֵחָקֵ֑ר כִּ֤י רַבּוּ֙ מֵֽאַרְבֶּ֔ה וְאֵ֥ין לָהֶ֖ם מִסְפָּֽר׃
verses.king_james: They shall cut down her forest, saith the Lord, though it cannot be searched; because they are more than the grasshoppers, and are innumerable.

verses.latin_vulgate: Confusa est filia Ægypti,  et tradita in manu populi aquilonis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֹבִ֖ישָׁה בַּת־מִצְרָ֑יִם נִתְּנָ֖ה בְּיַ֥ד עַם־צָפֽוֹן׃
verses.king_james: The daughter of Egypt shall be confounded; she shall be delivered into the hand of the people of the north.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit Dominus exercituum, Deus Israël :  Ecce ego visitabo super tumultum Alexandriæ,  et super Pharaonem, et super Ægyptum,  et super deos ejus, et super reges ejus,  et super Pharaonem, et super eos qui confidunt in eo :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָמַר֩ יְהֹוָ֨ה צְבָא֜וֹת אֱלֹהֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֗ל הִנְנִ֤י פוֹקֵד֙ אֶל־אָמ֣וֹן מִנֹּ֔א וְעַל־פַּרְעֹה֙ וְעַל־מִצְרַ֔יִם וְעַל־אֱלֹהֶ֖יהָ וְעַל־מְלָכֶ֑יהָ וְעַ֨ל־פַּרְעֹ֔ה וְעַ֥ל הַבֹּטְחִ֖ים בּֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: The Lord of hosts, the God of Israel, saith; Behold, I will punish the multitude of No, and Pharaoh, and Egypt, with their gods, and their kings; even Pharaoh, and all them that trust in him:

verses.latin_vulgate: et dabo eos in manu quærentium animam eorum,  et in manu Nabuchodonosor regis Babylonis,  et in manu servorum ejus :  et post hæc habitabitur sicut diebus pristinis, ait Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּנְתַתִּ֗ים בְּיַד֙ מְבַקְשֵׁ֣י נַפְשָׁ֔ם וּבְיַ֛ד נְבוּכַדְרֶאצַּ֥ר מֶלֶךְ־בָּבֶ֖ל וּבְיַד־עֲבָדָ֑יו וְאַחֲרֵי־כֵ֛ן תִּשְׁכֹּ֥ן כִּֽימֵי־קֶ֖דֶם נְאֻם־יְהֹוָֽה׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: And I will deliver them into the hand of those that seek their lives, and into the hand of Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon, and into the hand of his servants: and afterward it shall be inhabited, as in the days of old, saith the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et tu ne timeas, serve meus Jacob,  et ne paveas, Israël :  quia ecce ego salvum te faciam de longinquo,  et semen tuum de terra captivitatis tuæ :  et revertetur Jacob, et requiescet,  et prosperabitur, et non erit qui exterreat eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְ֠אַתָּ֠ה אַל־תִּירָ֞א עַבְדִּ֤י יַֽעֲקֹב֙ וְאַל־תֵּחַ֣ת יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל כִּ֠י הִנְנִ֤י מוֹשִֽׁעֲךָ֙ מֵרָח֔וֹק וְאֶֽת־זַרְעֲךָ֖ מֵאֶ֣רֶץ שִׁבְיָ֑ם וְשָׁ֧ב יַעֲק֛וֹב וְשָׁקַ֥ט וְשַׁאֲנַ֖ן וְאֵ֥ין מַחֲרִֽיד׃
verses.king_james: But fear not thou, O my servant Jacob, and be not dismayed, O Israel: for, behold, I will save thee from afar off, and thy seed from the land of their captivity; and Jacob shall return, and be in rest and at ease, and none shall make him afraid.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et tu noli timere, serve meus Jacob, ait Dominus,  quia tecum ego sum :  quia ego consumam cunctas gentes ad quas ejeci te,  te vero non consumam :  sed castigabo te in judicio,  nec quasi innocenti parcam tibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַ֠תָּ֠ה אַל־תִּירָ֞א עַבְדִּ֤י יַֽעֲקֹב֙ נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֔ה כִּ֥י אִתְּךָ֖ אָ֑נִי כִּי֩ אֶעֱשֶׂ֨ה כָלָ֜ה בְּכׇֽל־הַגּוֹיִ֣ם ׀ אֲשֶׁ֧ר הִדַּחְתִּ֣יךָ שָּׁ֗מָּה וְאֹֽתְךָ֙ לֹא־אֶעֱשֶׂ֣ה כָלָ֔ה וְיִסַּרְתִּ֙יךָ֙ לַמִּשְׁפָּ֔ט וְנַקֵּ֖ה לֹ֥א אֲנַקֶּֽךָּ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Fear thou not, O Jacob my servant, saith the Lord: for I am with thee; for I will make a full end of all the nations whither I have driven thee: but I will not make a full end of thee, but correct thee in measure; yet will I not leave thee wholly unpunished.
